title: Ink on Polyester (Generic)
timeEstimate: 20–60 minutes; identify ink type first; 91% IPA cloth tamp from BACK with transfer pad
stainChemistry: Ink on polyester is concentrated dye plus a solvent base — the type of ink determines the right solvent. Ballpoint = oil + dye + resin (alcohol works). Gel pen = water + pigment (Dawn often enough). Permanent marker = strong solvent + dye + resin (alcohol partial, honest cap real). Fountain pen = water + dye (cold water + Dawn). The recurring trap on polyester is hot water (sets the dye into the fiber permanently — polyester is industrially dyed with disperse dyes at high heat, same chemistry locks ink). The other trap is the HAIRSPRAY MYTH — modern hairsprays are mostly polymer + fragrance, leave a sticky residue that makes the stain worse. Plain 91% isopropyl alcohol on a cloth is the right solvent for most ink types.
whyThisWorks: Identify the ink type to pick the right solvent. 91% isopropyl alcohol on a cloth dissolves the ballpoint resin and lifts dye onto the cloth. Working from the BACK with a clean transfer pad cloth underneath pushes ink OUT instead of deeper. Cool wash plus AIR-DRY locks nothing. Stop if the spot starts SPREADING — some dyed-polyester combinations bleed under alcohol and continuing makes it worse.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): ballpoint = dye + oil-based vehicle + resin binder; gel pen = pigment + water-glycol; permanent marker = solvent dye + resin in xylene/toluene base; fountain pen = water-based dye. Pro uses VDS / amyl acetate per ink type + NSD + sodium hydrosulfite for residual chromophore on white polyester.
defaultAssumption: Pen leak / writing accident on a polyester garment. Identify ink type first — protocol varies. White polyester = real shot. Dyed performance polyester = hidden test mandatory. Hot-dried = often permanent.

spottingProtocol.equipment: The pen if available, good light
spottingProtocol.instruction: Ballpoint (most common, oily texture) = 91% IPA. Gel pen (smooth, water-based) = Dawn often enough. Permanent marker (Sharpie) = 91% IPA, honest cap real. Fountain pen (water-based) = cold water + Dawn. If unknown, treat as ballpoint.
Don't reach for hairspray — modern formulas are mostly polymer + fragrance, leave a sticky residue that makes the stain worse. Use plain 91% isopropyl alcohol from the drugstore.
Inside seam: dab 91% IPA, blot with white cloth. Color transfers = skip alcohol — pro from there. White / light polyester usually passes; dyed performance polyester sometimes doesn't.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Stack of clean white paper towels
spottingProtocol.instruction: Place a clean white cloth or stack of paper towels UNDER the stained area, ink-side facing the cloth. Work from the BACK. Ink transfers downward into the cloth instead of deeper into fiber.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 91% IPA on white cotton cloth, ventilation
spottingProtocol.instruction: Apply 91% IPA to a clean white cloth — never pour on garment. Tamp the BACK of the stain. Watch the cloth underneath — replace as it picks up ink. STOP if spot is SPREADING (polyester dye bleed). 2–4 passes for ballpoint; 4–6 for permanent marker.
Dawn worked into a damp cloth, press the spot 5 min to clear alcohol residue. Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

steps: 1. Identify ink type — ballpoint / gel / permanent marker / fountain.
3. Hidden-area IPA test on dyed polyester.
4. Back-side cloth setup as transfer pad.
5. 91% IPA cloth tamp from BACK, watch for spreading.
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat locks ink into polyester permanently
warnings: Identify ink type FIRST — protocol varies
NEVER hairspray — sticky polymer residue makes stain worse
NEVER hot water or hot dryer — locks ink permanently
STOP if spot is SPREADING — pro from there
Always blot, never rub — rubbing spreads dye
Hidden test on dyed polyester before alcohol
Work from BACK with cloth underneath
Permanent marker has honest cap — partial removal often the norm

homeSolutions.agent: Identify the Ink — Pick the Right Solvent
homeSolutions.instruction: Ink protocol varies by type. Ballpoint (oily, most common) = 91% isopropyl alcohol. Gel pen (smooth, water-based) = Dawn often enough. Permanent marker (Sharpie) = 91% IPA with honest expectation cap (50–70% cleared with shadow). Fountain pen (water-based) = cold water + Dawn. If unknown, treat as ballpoint. Two hard rules: NO hairspray (modern formulas leave sticky residue that makes stain worse) and NO hot water / hot dryer (locks ink into polyester permanently).

Before alcohol on dyed polyester: dab 91% IPA on an inside seam, blot with a white cloth. Color transfers = skip alcohol, pro from there. White / light polyester usually passes; dyed athletic polyester sometimes doesn't.

homeSolutions.agent: Set Up Cloth Underneath — Transfer Pad
homeSolutions.instruction: Place a stack of clean white paper towels UNDERNEATH the stained area. Work from the BACK of the garment, ink-side facing the cloth. Ink needs somewhere to go — the cloth catches the transfer.

homeSolutions.agent: 91% Isopropyl Alcohol on a Cloth — Tamp from Back
homeSolutions.instruction: Apply 91% isopropyl alcohol to a clean white cloth — never pour on the garment. Tamp the BACK of the stain. Watch the cloth underneath — replace it as it picks up ink. STOP if the spot starts SPREADING outward — that's polyester dye bleeding too, and continuing makes it worse. 2 to 4 passes for ballpoint; 4 to 6 for permanent marker (more passes, honest cap real). Open windows — alcohol is flammable, no flame.
Dawn worked into a damp cloth, press the spot 5 minutes to clear alcohol residue. Cool machine wash. AIR-DRY only. NEVER hot dryer until cleared.

Air-dry, then inspect in natural daylight. Faint shadow remaining = repeat the alcohol pass before any dryer heat. Permanent marker honest cap: 50–70% cleared with shadow is the typical home outcome on dyed polyester. Stop, accept, or escalate to pro for valuable garments.

steps: 1. Identify ink type — ballpoint / gel / permanent marker / fountain.
2. NO hairspray. NO hot water. NO hot dryer until cleared.
3. Hidden-area IPA test on dyed polyester.
4. Stack of paper towels UNDER the stain — transfer pad.
5. 91% IPA on cloth, tamp from BACK, watch for spreading.
6. Dawn damp cloth + cool machine wash.
7. AIR-DRY + daylight inspect — accept honest cap on permanent marker.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: STOP if the spot is SPREADING during alcohol contact. Stop on hot-dried ink (often permanent on polyester). Stop on dry-clean-only / specialty / performance polyester (pro). Stop after 6 passes plateau — accept honest cap on permanent marker.
warnings: Identify ink type FIRST — protocol varies
NEVER hairspray — sticky polymer residue makes stain worse
NEVER hot water or hot dryer until cleared
STOP if spreading — pro from there
Hidden test on dyed performance polyester
Work from BACK with cloth underneath
Permanent marker honest cap: 50–70% cleared with shadow

neverDo: Never use hairspray on ink — myth, sticky polymer residue
Never hot water on ink + polyester
Never alcohol on dyed performance polyester without hidden test
Never continue if spot is SPREADING
fiberSensitivities: Dyed performance / athletic polyester: hidden alcohol test mandatory; sometimes pro
Specialty performance fabric (compression, technical, Gore-Tex): pro — coatings react unpredictably
Polyester locks ink under heat — strict cool-only discipline
Permanent marker has honest cap — accept faint shadow or escalate

materialWarnings: Identify ink type FIRST — wrong solvent doesn't work or makes worse
Hairspray is the wrong solvent — modern formulas worsen stain
Stop if spreading — escalate, don't push
No hot water / no hot dryer — polyester locks ink
Permanent marker honest cap: faint shadow often remains

escalation.when: Spot is SPREADING during alcohol contact. Hot-dried ink (often permanent). Specialty / performance / dry-clean-only polyester. Hidden test fails on dyed polyester. Hairspray was already used (sticky residue blocks pro cleaning). Permanent marker honest cap reached on valuable garment.
escalation.whatToTell: "Ink on [white / dyed / athletic / specialty] polyester, type [ballpoint / gel / permanent marker / fountain], age [fresh / hours / hot-dried suspected], home-treated with [91% IPA cloth tamp from back, N passes + Dawn + cool wash + air-dry]. No hairspray, no hot. [Residual shadow / spreading observed / hot-dried suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner with solvent spotting capability

contextQuestions: Ballpoint, gel pen, permanent marker, or fountain pen?
Fresh, hours old, or hot-dried already?
Has any other product been tried (hairspray, peroxide)?
Is the spot spreading or holding shape?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Ink type determines solvent. The hairspray myth is the most common home failure across all ink types. Spreading during alcohol contact is the early warning of polyester dye bleed. Hot-dried ink on polyester is usually permanent. Permanent marker has the explicit honest cap (50–70% with shadow); pro for valuable garments. Specialty performance polyester (Gore-Tex, compression) goes to pro because the fabric coatings react unpredictably.
